rulings.Case Title: LUIS GONZALEZ VS. ANDREA DEBERRY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Contest Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 23, 2011, Line 1, DEFENDANT ANDREA DEBERRY's Motion To Contest Good Faith Settlement Determination. The motion is granted. The Court finds that the settlement is "in the ballpark" under Tech-Bilt. The undisputed evidence establishes that settling party (Bruce Parsley) was basically insolvent at the time of settlement. Further, at the time settlement was reached in 2009, the remaining co-defendants had obtained summary judgment; they were not present and did not participate in the settlement conference. There is no evidence of collusion. Although the dollar amount is small, it represented at the time of settlement 100% of the recovery plaintiff would receive, given the summary judgment rulings as to the other co-defendants. The Court concludes that the settlement was in good faith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MORVEN LIBURD VS. BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion For Application For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 23, 2011, Line 2, DEFENDANT QUORUM CONSTRUCTION CA,INC. Notice Of Motion And Otion For Application For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement. Late tentative ruling: Granted. The settlement meets the Tech Bilt factors and the amount is reasonable and in good faith within the meaning of CCP §§ 877 and 877.6 (See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488.) Settlor was not plaintiff's employer, and contends additionally that it has no liability under Privette (a motion for summary judgment is pending) and that plaintiff was negligent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SHABAN MOHAMED VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 23, 2011, Line 10, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Defendant met its initial burden of production in making a prima facie showing that Officer Canedo's use of force was objectively reasonable (see Canedo Decl. 3-10). The burden shifted to plaintiff to produce evidence showing the existence of a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff failed to do so. Plaintiff's declaration does not recite any facts and it is the only declaration supporting the opposition to the motion. Plaintiff's exhibits are unidentified and not authenticated. All of Plaintiff's Evidentiary Objections are sustained in their entirety. Plaintiff's belated request to amend his complaint or his opposition is denied. Defendant has shown that the motion for summary judgment is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
